response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software developer, but the profile fundamentally mismatches this role. The position hard-requires native-level Japanese, which the resume does not list (English, Romanian, German only). Core technical requirements are also absent: his C experience is limited to a brief 7-month stint in 2014-2015 (C is not even in his skills list), and there is no evidence of Linux kernel source modification, embedded firmware, L2/L3 switching, or security technologies such as MACsec, 802.1X, or encryption/key-exchange implementation. His strengths lie in web/full-stack TypeScript and D-language work, which this embedded security role does not need.
strengths: Long, continuous software engineering career (15+ years) with senior/staff-level roles
Systems-level exposure through low-latency DLang services, Linux, and some C/C++ IDE component work
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and knowledge sharing
concerns: No native-level Japanese proficiency, an explicit hard requirement
Minimal C-for-UNIX experience and no Linux kernel investigation or modification background
No embedded networking, L2/L3 switching, or security-protocol (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption) experience

key_factors.factor: Lack of embedded C, kernel, and network-security experience
Solid general seniority and engineering craftsmanship
